articles.title: Training your PMs on AI tools is the easy part
articles.summary: Training PMs to use AI is just the start. Without leadership support for experimentation, protected time, and psychological safety, AI merely accelerates unvalidated output rather than creating genuine customer value.

articles.title: The Architecture of Focus
articles.summary: Great work requires sustained focus. The best organizations protect attention by designing schedules and environments that make deep work the default.

articles.title: Software Is Not A Single-Player Game
articles.summary: Building durable software is a team effort, not a linear process from strategy to implementation. AI makes changes easier to produce, so collaboration should focus on reviewing actual code rather than upstream documents.

articles.title: Here's The Rub: We Don't Believe You
articles.summary: Senior leadership shouldn't blindly trust new leaders' assessments, as they may overlook underlying issues. The author suggests a communication cadence: recurring 1:1s across the ecosystem, weekly staff meetings, extended staff meetings to troubleshoot, and All Hands meetings to share findings.

articles.title: How to Make AI Agents Follow Your Design System
articles.summary: Large rules files don't ensure AI coding agents follow design systems, as prose is often outcompeted by legacy code. Instead, enforce design practices through code so failures appear directly in the agent's feedback loop.

articles.title: How Vanguard is embedding AI across the product lifecycle
articles.summary: Vanguard is integrating AI across its entire product development lifecycle, not just in software engineering. They've introduced a maturity model covering six key dimensions, including AI-powered delivery and team autonomy, sharing lessons from their implementation across 800+ product teams.

articles.title: The Hidden Structures of Problems
articles.summary: Problems frequently possess underlying, recurrent structures that clarify their complexity or the reasons they persist when approached without sufficient insight. This article introduces 31 distinct problem archetypes designed to help readers identify these patterns and foresee potential failure modes.

articles.title: Anthropic's Safety Superpower
articles.summary: Anthropic's safety mission strengthens its business while expanding its control over advanced AI. That alignment is powerful, but dangerous when one company believes it should decide what is best for everyone.

articles.title: Doing everyone else's job
articles.summary: Doing others' work helps you learn organizational systems, unblock priorities, and earn professional rewards.

articles.title: Every Frame Perfect
articles.summary: Every frame of an interface should feel intentional.
